mrcommons 1.67.2
  • Reference

Split Biomass into modern and traditional

Source: R/toolSplitBiomass.R
toolSplitBiomass.Rd

We assume that below a given GDP/cap level, all biomass is traditional and above a higher limit, all biomass is modern with a linear transition between the limits.

Usage

toolSplitBiomass(
  x,
  gdppop,
  split = "biomass",
  into = c("biotrad", "biomod"),
  dim = 3.1,
  limits = c(12300, 18500)
)

Arguments

x

MagPIE object including biomass data

gdppop

MagPIE object with GDP/cap data

split

character, name of item to split

into

character vector of length two with the names of the split items

dim

dimension of x with the item to split

limits

numeric vector of length two with the corresponding GDP/cap limits. The default values used to be 10k and 15k USD/cap converted from 2005 to 2017 dollars.

Value

MagPIE object including split biomass data

Author

Robin Hasse

On this page

Developed by Benjamin Leon Bodirsky, Kristine Karstens, Lavinia Baumstark, Isabelle Weindl, Xiaoxi Wang, Abhijeet Mishra, Stephen Wirth, Mishko Stevanovic, Nele Steinmetz, Ulrich Kreidenweis, Renato Rodrigues, Roman Popov, Florian Humpenoeder, Anastasis Giannousakis, Antoine Levesque, David Klein, Ewerton Araujo, Eva Bleidorn, Felicitas Beier, Julian Oeser, Michaja Pehl, Debbora Leip, Michael Crawford, Edna Molina Bacca, Patrick von Jeetze, Eleonora Martinelli, Felix Schreyer, Bjoern Soergel, Pascal Sauer, David Hötten, Robin Hasse, Gabriel Abrahão, Pascal Weigmann, Jan Philipp Dietrich.
